name: CKAD Knowledge Graph
description: Knowledge graph for CKAD exam preparation. Nodes: categories -> exercises -> leaf tasks. Leaf tasks are simple tasks that can be completed with a single command. Each leaf task references a deduplicated flashcard in flashcards.json via flashcardId.

exercises.title: Create a namespace called 'mynamespace' and a pod with image nginx called nginx on this namespace

tasks.task: Create a namespace called mynamespace
Create a pod named nginx with image nginx in namespace mynamespace

exercises.title: Create the pod that was just described using YAML

tasks.task: Generate pod YAML without creating the pod (dry-run)
tasks.command: kubectl run nginx --image=nginx --restart=Never --dry-run=client -n mynamespace -o yaml > pod.yaml

tasks.task: Create a resource from a YAML manifest file

exercises.title: Create a busybox pod (using kubectl command) that runs the command 'env'. Run it and see the output

tasks.task: Create a busybox pod that runs the command env
See the pod output in its logs

exercises.title: Create a busybox pod (using YAML) that runs the command 'env'. Run it and see the output

tasks.task: Generate YAML for a pod that runs a command, without creating it
tasks.command: kubectl run busybox --image=busybox --restart=Never --dry-run=client -o yaml --command -- env > envpod.yaml

tasks.task: Apply the manifest and check the logs

tasks.task: Change the image of a running pod
Describe the pod to see events (container killed and recreated)
Watch the pod status changes
Check the pod's image using jsonpath

tasks.task: Get pod IPs (wide output)

Run a temporary busybox pod and wget the nginx pod IP

tasks.task: Open an interactive shell in a running pod

exercises.title: Create a busybox pod that echoes 'hello world' and then exits

tasks.task: Run a one-shot pod with arguments and see its output

exercises.title: Do the same, but have the pod deleted automatically when it's completed

tasks.task: Run a temporary pod that is deleted automatically after it exits

exercises.title: Create an nginx pod and set an env value as 'var1=val1'. Check the env value existence within the pod

tasks.task: Create a pod with an environment variable
Print environment variables inside a running pod

exercises.title: Create a Pod with two containers, both with image busybox and command 'echo hello; sleep 3600'. Connect to the second container and run 'ls'

tasks.task: Generate a single-container pod YAML with the command as a base (dry-run)
tasks.command: kubectl run busybox --image=busybox --restart=Never -o yaml --dry-run=client -- /bin/sh -c 'echo hello;sleep 3600' > pod.yaml

tasks.task: Edit the YAML to add a second busybox container (different name) to the containers list
Create the pod from the manifest
Run a command in a specific container of a multi-container pod

exercises.title: Create a pod with an nginx container exposed on port 80. Add a busybox init container which writes 'Test' to /work-dir/index.html. Share an emptyDir volume mounted at /usr/share/nginx/html (nginx) and /work-dir (init container). Get the pod IP and wget it from a busybox pod

tasks.task: Generate a base pod YAML with container port 80 (dry-run)
Edit the YAML to add an emptyDir volume and mount it in the nginx container at /usr/share/nginx/html
Edit the YAML to add a busybox init container that writes the file and mounts the volume at /work-dir
Apply the pod manifest
Get the pod IP (wide output)
Run a temporary busybox pod and wget the pod IP
tasks.command: kubectl run box-test --image=busybox --restart=Never -it --rm -- /bin/sh -c "wget -O- $(kubectl get pod box -o jsonpath='{.status.podIP}')"

tasks.task: Add the accelerator label to a node
Edit the pod YAML to add a nodeSelector for the accelerator label
Find where nodeSelector goes in the pod spec
Alternative: edit the pod YAML to use required node affinity instead of nodeSelector

tasks.task: Edit the pod YAML to set spec.nodeName to node01
Verify which node the pod landed on

exercises.title: Taint a node with key 'tier' and value 'frontend' with the effect 'NoSchedule'. Then, create a pod that tolerates this taint

tasks.task: Taint the node (and view taints with kubectl describe node)
Edit the pod YAML to add a toleration for the taint
Create the pod from the manifest

exercises.title: Implement canary deployment by running two instances of nginx marked as version=v1 and version=v2 so that the load is balanced at 75%-25% ratio

tasks.task: Write the v1 deployment (3 replicas, labels app=my-app + version=v1) and the service selecting only app=my-app
Test the service from a temporary busybox pod
Write and deploy the v2 deployment (1 replica, labels app=my-app + version=v2)
Observe the load balancing across versions from a temporary pod
tasks.command: kubectl run -it --rm --restart=Never busybox --image=gcr.io/google-containers/busybox -- /bin/sh -c 'while sleep 1; do wget -qO- my-app-svc; done'

tasks.task: Promote v2 by scaling it up
Shut down v1

exercises.title: Create a job named pi with image perl:5.34 that runs the command with arguments "perl -Mbignum=bpi -wle 'print bpi(2000)'"

tasks.task: Create the job imperatively
tasks.command: kubectl create job pi --image=perl:5.34 -- perl -Mbignum=bpi -wle 'print bpi(2000)'

exercises.title: Wait till it's done, get the output

tasks.task: Wait for the job to complete
Get the job output from its logs
Delete the job

exercises.title: Create a job with the image busybox that executes the command 'echo hello;sleep 30;echo world'

tasks.task: Create the job with a shell command
tasks.command: kubectl create job busybox --image=busybox -- /bin/sh -c 'echo hello;sleep 30;echo world'

exercises.title: Follow the logs for the pod (you'll wait for 30 seconds)

tasks.task: Follow the logs of the job's pod

exercises.title: See the status of the job, describe it and see the logs

tasks.task: See the job status
Describe the job
See the job logs

exercises.title: Delete the job

tasks.task: Delete the job

exercises.title: Create the same job, make it run 5 times, one after the other. Verify its status and delete it

tasks.task: Generate the job YAML without creating it (dry-run)
tasks.command: kubectl create job busybox --image=busybox --dry-run=client -o yaml -- /bin/sh -c 'echo hello;sleep 30;echo world' > job.yaml

tasks.task: Edit the YAML to add completions: 5 (and completionMode: Indexed)
Create the job from the manifest
Watch the job until 5 completions
Delete the job

exercises.title: Create the same job, but make it run 5 parallel times

tasks.task: Edit the YAML to add parallelism: 5
Create the job from the manifest
Delete the job

exercises.title: Create a job but ensure that it will be automatically terminated by kubernetes if it takes more than 30 seconds to execute

tasks.task: Generate the job YAML without creating it (dry-run)
tasks.command: kubectl create job busybox --image=busybox --dry-run=client -o yaml -- /bin/sh -c 'while true; do echo hello; sleep 10;done' > job.yaml

tasks.task: Edit the YAML to add activeDeadlineSeconds: 30 to the job spec

exercises.title: Create a cron job with image busybox that runs on a schedule of "*/1 * * * *" and writes 'date; echo Hello from the Kubernetes cluster' to standard output

tasks.task: Create the cron job imperatively
tasks.command: kubectl create cronjob busybox --image=busybox --schedule="*/1 * * * *" -- /bin/sh -c 'date; echo Hello from the Kubernetes cluster'

exercises.title: See its logs and delete it

tasks.task: Read the logs of the pod created by the cron job
Delete the cron job

exercises.title: Create the same cron job again, and watch the status. Once it ran, check which job ran by the created cron job. Check the log, and delete the cron job

tasks.task: List the cron jobs
Watch the jobs created by the cron job
See that the pods carry a label naming their parent job
Read the logs of the pod
Delete the cron job

exercises.title: Create a cron job that runs every minute; it should be terminated if it takes more than 17 seconds to start execution after its scheduled time

tasks.task: Generate the cron job YAML without creating it (dry-run)
tasks.command: kubectl create cronjob time-limited-job --image=busybox --restart=Never --dry-run=client --schedule="* * * * *" -o yaml -- /bin/sh -c 'date; echo Hello from the Kubernetes cluster' > time-limited-job.yaml

tasks.task: Edit the YAML to add startingDeadlineSeconds: 17 to the cronjob spec

exercises.title: Create a cron job that runs every minute; it should be terminated if it successfully starts but takes more than 12 seconds to complete execution

tasks.task: Generate the cron job YAML without creating it (dry-run)
tasks.command: kubectl create cronjob time-limited-job --image=busybox --restart=Never --dry-run=client --schedule="* * * * *" -o yaml -- /bin/sh -c 'date; echo Hello from the Kubernetes cluster' > time-limited-job.yaml

tasks.task: Edit the YAML to add activeDeadlineSeconds: 12 at spec.jobTemplate.spec

exercises.title: Create a job from cronjob

tasks.task: Create a one-off job from an existing cron job
